name: Cover Drive
description: The signature Kohli picture: head still, bat flowing through extra cover, balance never breaking.
explanation: Built on late contact and supreme alignment, this stroke turns the off-side corridor into a scoring invitation.

name: Straight Drive
description: Minimal flourish, maximum purity. The ball leaves the bat like it has been ordered to obey.
explanation: This is Kohli's line-through-the-ball shot, powered by precise stride length and a straight extension.

name: Off Drive
description: A tighter, more surgical cousin of the cover drive, used when line discipline is strong.
explanation: He stays inside the ball just enough to keep it grounded and keep the field honest.

name: On Drive
description: A less discussed but beautifully balanced stroke through mid-on.
explanation: The shot appears when his front hip clears cleanly and the wrists stay soft through impact.

name: Flick
description: Kohli's wrists turn risk into elegance on the leg side.
explanation: The trigger movement lines him up early enough to close the bat face with complete control.

name: Leg Glance
description: A late, precise redirection that punishes anything drifting too straight.
explanation: He keeps the movement tiny and lets timing do almost all the work.

name: Pull Shot
description: A statement shot when bowlers try to crowd him with pace.
explanation: Kohli's best pulls come when his base stays quiet and the shoulders unwind late.

name: Hook Shot
description: Less frequent than the pull, but useful when bounce climbs into his upper body.
explanation: He picks this only when he trusts the height, which is why the shot feels selective rather than reckless.

name: Square Cut
description: A back-foot answer to width, played with less violence than perfect timing.
explanation: His narrower stance evolution made this shot cleaner and more available.

name: Late Cut
description: More touch than force, used to turn third man into a scoring option.
explanation: The late cut reflects how still his head can remain even when his hands manipulate angles.

name: Sweep
description: A controlled answer to spin rather than a foundational scoring pattern.
explanation: He uses it as a field manipulator and rhythm breaker against spin-heavy plans.

name: Reverse Sweep
description: Not central to his identity, but valuable as a modern disruption shot.
explanation: Its value lies in forcing spinners and captains to respect width behind point.

name: Lofted Drive
description: The classical foundation lifted into a white-ball scoring weapon.
explanation: Because his base stays steady, even the aerial version still looks balanced.

name: Inside-Out Drive
description: A premium answer to spin, especially when the infield squeezes conventional drives.
explanation: His feet create the angle first, allowing the bat to stay clean and tall through the line.

name: Paddle Scoop
description: A modern release valve, used more selectively than by pure T20 improvisers.
explanation: When he plays it, the movement is late enough to disguise the intent until the final moment.

name: Upper Cut
description: A response to pace and bounce directed outside off.
explanation: The upper cut is most effective when the hands stay high and relaxed through the release point.

name: Back Foot Punch
description: Fast hands, little fuss, and plenty of authority through point and cover.
explanation: This stroke became cleaner as his stance evolved and his balance over the back foot improved.

name: Wrist Flick
description: A wrist-led extension of the leg-side game, especially against straight pace.
explanation: This is where his hand speed and control become almost impossible to separate.

name: Pick Up Shot
description: A leg-side power release when bowlers miss the yorker or over-correct straighter.
explanation: He relies on base stability to turn a narrow scoring window into a clean boundary shot.

name: Slog Sweep
description: An uncommon but useful power option against spin when field depth invites it.
explanation: Kohli plays it with a cleaner base than most, even when the intent is pure elevation.

name: Controlled Pull
description: Less brute force, more placement and command into the leg-side pockets.
explanation: He rolls the wrists well enough to keep this aggressive response lower-risk.

name: Running Glide
description: A modern soft-hands deflection that pairs perfectly with his running between wickets.
explanation: This shot embodies his strike-rotation intelligence more than raw highlight-making flair.

name: Check Drive
description: A half-committed-looking shot that is actually meticulously controlled.
explanation: He keeps the shape compact, guiding rather than fully releasing the arms.

name: Punch Through Covers
description: An in-between stroke that sits between drive and cut, played off a slightly shorter length.
explanation: This is one of the easiest ways to see his hands and balance working in the same sentence.

name: Signature Kohli Cover Drive
description: The one shot that became branding, memory, and emotion all at once.
explanation: Everything comes together here: head stillness, stride shape, hand release, and the confidence to hit the same scoring lane again and again.
